title: Today's AI Brief: New Moms Are Returning to Coding Jobs Radically Reshaped by AI
summary: New moms return to coding jobs reshaped by AI, Corporate America faces AI reckoning
imageAlt: Image from source article for New Moms Are Returning to Coding Jobs Radically Reshaped by AI
imageCaption: New Moms Are Returning to Coding Jobs Radically Reshaped by AI
imageSourceTitle: New Moms Are Returning to Coding Jobs Radically Reshaped by AI

body.text: I'm looking at a couple of interesting developments in the world of AI. First, it seems that new moms are returning to coding jobs that have been radically reshaped by AI. According to an article in Wired, Danielle, a software developer, saw her profession undergo a drastic reinvention as she settled into new motherhood. This shift is not just about Danielle; it's about a larger trend of women returning to work after parental leave and finding that AI has changed the coding landscape.

The Wired article highlights that AI has transformed coding jobs in ways that make them more accessible to people who have been out of the workforce, like new moms. This is interesting because it shows how AI can create new opportunities for people who thought they were out of the game. It's like getting a second chance at a career, but with a new set of tools.

Meanwhile, Axios reports that Corporate America is entering its AI reckoning. This means that companies are finally figuring out how to deal with the impact of AI on their businesses. It's a bit like the calm after the storm; companies are realizing that AI is not just a buzzword, but a real force that's changing how they operate.

My quick read: the artificial intelligence story keeps getting bigger, but also stranger in very human ways. One moment it is strategy and infrastructure; the next, the Vatican is in the room. That is a pretty good sign this technology has left the lab and wandered into the town square.
